ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for calamari juice or squid-based beverage. FOODON_03301710 (squid food product) is the closest parent category available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for squid juice as a beverage. A0CJL (Cephalopods and products thereof) is the closest defensible parent code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for calamari juice or squid-based beverage product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 240 g (one cup or glass), as typically served as a beverage at Malaysian and Singaporean food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Squid, raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 15149) — adapted to juice/broth form with dilution factor applied
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Calamari juice is a dilute aqueous extract of squid. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA FoodData Central for calamari juice as a beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by applying an approximate 15–20% dilution factor to USDA SR Legacy squid (raw) values, reflecting the liquid extraction process. Protein and fat are substantially reduced relative to whole squid. Sodium is retained from the cooking liquid. Values represent a conservative best-estimate and are flagged as low confidence.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of protein- and fat-dominant foods — University of Sydney GI Database convention for near-zero carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Calamari juice contains approximately 0.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g and negligible sugar. No direct GI measurement exists for calamari juice. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention, foods with negligible glycemic carbohydrate content are assigned a GI of effectively zero; the value is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive integer) per Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule. Glycemic load per 240 g serving = (1 × 1.2) / 100 ≈ 0.0, rounded to 0.0. gi_category is low.

hypertension.guidance: Calamari juice may contain approximately 180 mg sodium per 100 g (432 mg per 240 g serving), which represents a meaningful sodium load for individuals managing hypertension; portion size should be monitored and low-sodium preparation methods preferred.

type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 0.5 g carbohydrate and 0.0 g sugar per 100 g, calamari juice has a negligible glycemic impact and is unlikely to cause postprandial blood glucose elevation, making it a low-concern beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low fat and saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Calamari juice is very low in total fat (approximately 0.3 g per 100 g) and saturated fat (approximately 0.1 g per 100 g), posing minimal cardiovascular lipid risk; however, sodium content warrants attention in individuals with cardiovascular disease who require sodium restriction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Calamari juice is a seafood-based beverage made from the liquid extracted or derived from cooked squid (calamari), typically served warm or at room temperature as a drink at Malaysian and Singaporean food stalls and casual dining establishments. The beverage is produced by boiling or steaming squid and collecting the resulting broth or juice, which carries a mild, savoury, oceanic flavour characteristic of cephalopod seafood. The texture is thin and watery, similar to a light seafood broth, with a subtle umami quality derived from the natural amino acids and peptides released from squid muscle tissue during cooking. Calamari juice is low in calories (approximately 15 kcal per 100 g), contains a modest amount of protein (approximately 2.5 g per 100 g), and is very low in fat and carbohydrates, making it a light, protein-containing beverage. Its sodium content is notable due to the cooking process and any added salt. From a clinical perspective, calamari juice is low-glycemic and suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; however, its sodium content requires monitoring for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The beverage is free of gluten and dairy, and is halal-compliant. It is a niche item found primarily in Malaysian-influenced food culture in Singapore, often listed alongside other seafood drinks or soups on hawker stall menus.
common_user_queries: How many calories in calamari juice?
Is calamari juice good for diabetics?
What does calamari juice taste like?
Is squid juice the same as calamari juice?
Where can I find calamari juice in Singapore?
Is calamari juice high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for calamari juice exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated by applying a dilution factor to USDA SR Legacy squid (raw) data. The food is a niche beverage with limited published nutritional data. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: Calamari juice is a niche seafood beverage with no direct entry in any approved primary nutritional database. All per_100g values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy squid (raw) data with an approximate 15–20% dilution factor applied to reflect the liquid extraction process; these values carry inherent uncertainty and should be replaced with laboratory-analysed data if available. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source derivation. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source derivation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as squid is a lean seafood with negligible trans fat content (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy squid data). cholesterol_mg is -1 as it was not carried through the dilution estimation and requires direct measurement. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reliably estimable from the dilution model applied and are not reported for this specific beverage form in any approved source. sugar_g is 0.0 as squid contains no added or intrinsic sugars (naturally zero). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as this is a liquid extract with no fibre (naturally zero). glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. The food is tagged as Malaysian cuisine based on the field survey source data and the cuisine tags provided; however, calamari juice is also found in Singaporean hawker culture. Multilingual names follow the Singaporean dishes rule as the food is served in Singapore. The food_id GD002678 is preserved from the input seed record.
